title: Identity Expansion

content.statement: You must grow into the person who can hold your goals.
content.question: What identity do you need to grow into to hold the life you're designing?
sop: Write a specific description of who you need to become to hold your next goal, not vague ('successful'), specific ('the person who closes six-figure deals calmly').
Rate your current Identity Expansion, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), how much you've actually grown into that description versus just hoping to.
List 3 actions that version of you would take this week that current-you is avoiding.
Take one of those actions today, even if it feels premature.
Review monthly, identity expands through repeated action, not through deciding once.
questions: What identity do you need to grow into to hold the life you're designing?
Would the person you are today make the decisions the person you're trying to become would make?
What's one action this week that only the 'next-level' version of you would take?
content.equation_example: Identity Expansion (rate 1-10: how much you've grown into the next-level version of yourself) times Action (rate 1-10: how consistently you act as that version) equals Capacity. Identity Expansion 6 times Action 7 equals 42. Higher means you can actually hold bigger goals without reverting to old patterns; lower means your goals are outgrowing who you currently are.
content.closing_statement: Grow into the person before the goal requires it.

caption:
Principle Nº 186: Identity Expansion

You must grow into the person who can hold your goals.

Ask yourself: What identity do you need to grow into to hold the life you're designing?
